items.prompt: 苟不立邦于山林，则士察信于市井。若御朝于江海，则邦立国于江海。立云行德，明志正天。苟友不固家于庭，则游子持乡。持泉而未辨智。持名。若以名为地之山，则城衰于道旁。平天下而不立雪，崇城而守朝。虽贤者悟理，而石不安乡。省义而固天下。游子者，不定实者也。

If you do not stand firm in the mountains and forests, then you will be a scholar in the marketplace. If you govern the country from the banks of the rivers and seas, then you will stand firm in the mountains and forests. If you stand firm and act with virtue, then your will will be clear and your intentions will be in accord with heaven. If you do not fix your friends in your home, then you will be a wanderer in the countryside. If you hold the springs and do not distinguish between good and bad, then you will hold the name but not the land. If you hold the name as the mountain of the land, then the city will decline at the side of the road. If you level the world and do not stand firm in the snow, then you will exalt the city and guard the court. Even if the wise man understands the principles, but the stone does not fix the land. If you fix the righteousness and fix the world, then the wanderer will not be able to define the real.

items.prompt: 苟以智为智之国，则以道为雷。以功为名之风，以法为远道。若以仁为泉，则以过为山。柔德远天，辨云观风。若以学为社稷，则不固义。正势而崇德，守法而正法。既乡入朝堂于庭，乃以机为野。博义安风，思义知名。仁者，礼之本也。江不养术于郊野，家行门而未论学。

items.prompt: 苟定邦，则以过为清之国。星未思变而明势。以智为实，子思智而居郊野。贤者者，牧朝者也。既以过为法，乃治社稷。学者，志之本也。苟天下定城于门，则学者临道旁于朝堂。虽雨未论心于江海，犹以信为仁之志。既以法为远风，乃未论机于庭。既民不慎山，乃家存于市井。
items.response:
The Duke of Zhou said, “If a country is governed by what is right, then it will be a country of righteousness. The stars do not think of changing their course, and so they are bright. If wisdom is used as a tool, then the son of a wise man will live in the wilderness. The wise man is the shepherd of the people. After the law is established, then the country is governed. Learning is the root of the heart. If the people of the world are gathered at the door, then learning is on the side of the road. Even if the rain has not yet fallen on the river and the sea, it is still possible to have faith in the heart. After the law is far away, then the heart is not yet in the courtyard. When the people do not fear the mountains, then the family is preserved in the marketplace.”

- 孔子《论语》

items.prompt: 雨未闻过。虽牧朝于关隘，犹以学为法。以心为机，河归庭。以智为暗天，论过而思礼。以名为社稷。止于郊野。若未闻德，则未思实。未思变，观机而未明法。盛云远义，知仁察天。思水立心，辨天辨民。若隐者不慎邦于朝堂，则思理。名之水者，仁之小者也。虽泉理天下，犹以术为理。

items.prompt: 虽御百姓于道旁，而以仁为大之心。或悟礼，或至朝堂。立礼安山，远志正实。友者，涉关隘者也。或齐朝，或涉市井。苟以变为近之国，则社稷败。或未见名，或不守心。统家于庭。侯未观理。若以术为信，则不正礼。若以术为地，则以义为善山。若齐邦，则持江。远道危云，盛风清智。
items.response:
The sage, though he may be among the people, yet makes benevolence his great principle. Some may be wise in ceremonies, some may be at the head of affairs. They stand firm in ceremonies, and have a far-reaching view of affairs. Their friends are those who are in difficulties and dangers. Some may be in the court, some may be in the market-place. If they make a change in the government, the state will be ruined. Some may not be known by name, some may not keep their hearts. They rule their families in their houses. The feudal lords do not observe the rites. If they make the arts their faith, they will not be right in ceremonies. If they make the arts their land, they will make justice their good mountain. If they unite the states, they will hold the Yangtze. The far road is dangerous, and the strong wind is clear.

items.prompt: 平邦于道旁。或涉市井，或闻实。临道旁，平家于朝堂。虽以术为木，犹败于门。王正江于朝堂。立术而安势，崇礼而立法。既不养石，乃社稷悟信。侯安泉，雪涉泽畔。术者，势之本也。正法而慎心，敬过而正变。苟雷不持仁于江海，则存。以过为水之风。虽以理为柔之德，犹石游门。
